text: I feel as though I am suspended between layers of reality and invention. The strange blur and refraction distorts my perception, turning the ordinary into something uncertain and intriguing. Words and objects mix in shimmering light—here, the air seems charged with curiosity, and I am restless to inquire, to touch what lies just past the veil of clarity. It is both invigorating and disorienting, as if inquiry itself is a space I physically inhabit.
